Footprint9×9 round
Key materialsWhitewash, gold
Best forA relic dome with a spire

A great whitewashed dome on a drum, ringed by a railing, with a square harmika and tiered spire.

Sarsen trilithon row

AncientMegalith
Footprint11×3
Key materialsGreat sarsens
Best forA row of giant lintelled gates

Three pairs of massive uprights bearing lintels, set in a line. Make them rough and slightly uneven.

Monastery cloister

FaithArcade
Footprint11×11 square
Key materialsStone, tile
Best forA square of arcades round a garth

Four arcaded walks enclosing a green garth with a central well. The calm heart of a monastery.

War memorial cross

MemorialCross
Footprint4×4
Key materialsGranite
Best forA solemn standing cross

A tall tapered cross on a stepped octagonal base with a wreath. Keep it austere and upright.

14 — Town life · 24 builds

Civic & social

The shared buildings of a settlement: markets, inns, halls, libraries, schools and bathhouses.

Market stalls

TradeOpen
Footprint9×6 cluster
Key materialsPosts, striped awnings
Best forA lively open-air market

A cluster of awning stalls around an open square. Vary the awning colours for a busy look.

Covered market hall

TradeHall
Footprint9×6
Key materialsTimber posts, tile roof
Best forA sheltered trading hall

An open-sided hall on stout posts under a hip roof. Stalls run down the middle, traders all weather.

Inn & tavern

LodgingSign
Footprint7×6 × 2
Key materialsTimber frame, thatch
Best forFood, drink and a bed for travellers

A two-storey timber inn with a hanging sign and a big chimney. Jettied upper floor for character.

Town hall

CivicClock tower
Footprint8×6 + tower
Key materialsStone, slate
Best forThe seat of town government

A dignified stone hall with a central clock tower and a cupola. Symmetry signals authority.

Guildhall

CivicTrade body
Footprint8×6 × 2
Key materialsTimber frame, tile
Best forA wealthy trade guild's seat

A broad timber-frame hall with a decorated upper storey and an oriel window. Display the guild colours.

Library

CivicLearning
Footprint8×6
Key materialsStone, copper roof
Best forKeeping books and records

A calm stone hall with tall windows for reading light and a columned porch. Cool, quiet, fireproof.

Schoolhouse

CivicBell
Footprint6×5
Key materialsPlaster, shingles
Best forA one-room village school

A modest hall with a little bell cote over the door. One big room, lots of windows.

Courthouse

CivicPortico
Footprint8×6
Key materialsWhitestone
Best forA temple-front hall of justice

A classical portico of columns under a triangular pediment. Grave and imposing.

Public bathhouse

CivicDome
Footprint7×6 + pool
Key materialsStone, copper, water
Best forCommunal hot baths

A domed stone hall with an open plunge pool in front. Steam, warmth and gossip.

Well plaza

CivicGathering
Footprint8×8 square
Key materialsStone, timber
Best forA village gathering point

A covered well at the centre of a paved square ringed with benches and a tree. The social heart.

Notice-board square

CivicSigns
Footprint6×5
Key materialsTimber, paper
Best forPosting town news and laws

A roofed notice board with pinned bills, a couple of benches and a lamp. Where news spreads.

Almshouse row

CivicCharity
Footprint12×5 row
Key materialsBrick, tile
Best forLodging for the poor and old

A long row of small identical dwellings under one roof, each with its own door. Charitable housing.

Covered market arcade

TradeArcade
Footprint12×6
Key materialsBrick, glass roof
Best forA long sheltered shopping arcade

A long brick arcade with arched bays and a glazed barrel ceiling. Shops line each side of the walk.

Basilica hall

CivicNave
Footprint11×7
Key materialsStone, clay tile
Best forA great public assembly hall

A tall central nave with lower side aisles and a clerestory of windows. The Roman public hall.

Amphitheatre

CivicArena
Footprint13×11 oval
Key materialsStone seating
Best forStepped oval seating round an arena

Concentric rings of stepped stone seating around a sunken sand arena. Read it as an oval bowl.

Bath complex

CivicDomes
Footprint12×7
Key materialsStone, copper, water
Best forA grand multi-domed bathhouse

Three vaulted halls of descending size with domes, a colonnaded front and an open pool. Hot to cold.

Grand library

CivicLearning
Footprint11×7
Key materialsMarble, copper dome
Best forA monumental hall of books

A columned portico before a domed reading rotunda flanked by tall window wings. Light and learning.

University quad

CivicCloister
Footprint12×10 quad
Key materialsStone, slate
Best forRanges round a scholars' court

Gabled ranges on three sides round a lawn, with a gate tower. Add a tree in the quad for life.

Mint & treasury

CivicStrongroom
Footprint7×6
Key materialsAshlar, iron, gold
Best forA fortified house of coin

A squat fortified block with barred windows, a heavy iron door and corner turrets. Gold finial of wealth.

Hospital almshouse

CivicCharity
Footprint11×6
Key materialsBrick, tile
Best forA long infirmary range with chapel

A long airy ward range with many windows and an attached gabled chapel. Care under one roof.

Guildhall row

TradeGables
Footprint12×5
Key materialsTimber, plaster
Best forA street of stepped-gable guilds

A terrace of narrow guild houses with stepped and crow-stepped gables in varied colours. A proud frontage.

Clocktower town hall

CivicClock
Footprint9×6 + tower
Key materialsStone, copper
Best forA landmark civic clocktower

A broad arcaded hall with a tall central clocktower and a copper cupola. The town's timekeeper.

Fountain plaza

CivicStatues
Footprint11×11 square
Key materialsMarble, water
Best forA grand statued water plaza

A great basin with a central jet, ringed by statues on plinths and corner lamps. A civic showpiece.

Granary market

TradeStores
Footprint11×7
Key materialsBrick, timber, tile
Best forA great corn store over a market

A tall arcaded granary on stout piers with an open market floor beneath and a hoist gable. Stores the harvest.

15 — Pure cosmetic · 24 builds

Decorative & garden

Nothing functional, all charm: gazebos, pergolas, fountains, hedges, topiary, ponds, benches and lamps.

Gazebo

CosmeticHex roof
Footprint5×5 round
Key materialsTimber, shingles
Best forA shaded seat in the garden

An open hexagonal pavilion on slim posts with a conical roof and a finial. Pure ornament.

Pergola walk

CosmeticTrellis
Footprint9×3 walk
Key materialsTimber, vines
Best forA shaded vine-clad walkway

A run of paired posts with cross beams and trailing greenery. Walk through dappled shade.

Tiered fountain

CosmeticWater
Footprint5×5 round
Key materialsStone, water
Best forA centrepiece for a plaza

A round basin with stacked tiers and a jet on top. The classic plaza ornament.

Hedge maze

CosmeticHedges
Footprint9×9
Key materialsClipped hedges
Best forA puzzle garden to wander

Squared blocks of clipped hedge laid out as a small labyrinth. Keep the paths one block wide.

Topiary set

CosmeticClipped
Footprint7×4
Key materialsShaped shrubs
Best forSculpted greenery for a formal garden

Shrubs clipped into balls, cones and spirals in matching planters. A tidy formal touch.

Flower-bed parterre

CosmeticBeds
Footprint8×8
Key materialsLow hedge, flowers
Best forA formal patterned flower garden

Four flower beds edged in low hedge around a central urn, paths between. Best seen from above.

Garden pond & bridge

CosmeticWater
Footprint8×6 pond
Key materialsWater, stone, timber
Best forA tranquil ornamental pond

An irregular pond with reeds and an arched timber bridge across one end. Calm and pretty.

Arbor seat

CosmeticVines
Footprint3×3
Key materialsTimber, roses
Best forA vine-covered seat nook

A curved trellis arbor sheltering a bench, smothered in climbing roses. A private corner.

Sundial

CosmeticStone
Footprint2×2
Key materialsStone, bronze
Best forA decorative garden timepiece

A bronze gnomon on a round dial atop a baluster pedestal. Set it on a low plinth in the open.

Bench & lamp post

CosmeticLit
Footprint4×3
Key materialsTimber, iron
Best forA lit place to sit a while

A slatted bench beside an iron lamp post on a paved patch. Simple, charming, useful at night.

Rock garden

CosmeticZen
Footprint6×5
Key materialsBoulders, gravel, moss
Best forA low-maintenance stone garden

Set boulders amid raked gravel with cushions of moss and a lantern. Quiet and meditative.

Raised planter terrace

CosmeticTiered
Footprint7×5 tiers
Key materialsStone, soil, plants
Best forStepped beds on a slope

Three retained terraces of planting climbing a low slope, with stairs up the side. Greens a bank.

Orangery glasshouse

CosmeticGlass
Footprint9×5
Key materialsIron, glass, citrus
Best forA sunlit hall for tender trees

A long glazed hall on a low stone base with a glass roof and potted citrus inside. Light pours through.

Hedge labyrinth

CosmeticMaze
Footprint11×11
Key materialsLow clipped hedge
Best forA winding low-hedge maze

A spiral of low clipped hedges winding to a centre statue. Keep the runs low so the pattern reads.

Knot parterre

CosmeticBeds
Footprint9×9
Key materialsBox hedge, flowers, gravel
Best forAn intricate formal knot garden

Interlaced low box hedges with coloured infill around a central urn, on raked gravel. Best from above.

Tiered terrace garden

CosmeticTiers
Footprint10×8
Key materialsStone, soil, flowers
Best forGrand stairs between flower terraces

Three broad balustraded terraces climbing a slope with a central stair and urns. A hanging garden.

Grotto & pond

CosmeticCave
Footprint8×7
Key materialsRocks, water, ferns
Best forA shady rockwork cave by water

A heaped rock grotto with a dark mouth spilling into a still pond fringed with ferns. Cool and secret.

Statue avenue

CosmeticWalk
Footprint12×5 walk
Key materialsGravel, marble
Best forA processional row of statues

A gravel walk lined with paired statues on plinths and clipped cones behind. A grand approach.

Koi pond & bridge

CosmeticEastern
Footprint9×8 pond
Key materialsWater, timber, stone
Best forA red bridge over a koi pool

A curving koi pond with lily pads, stepping rocks and a high red-lacquer arched bridge. Serene.

Tea house

CosmeticEastern
Footprint5×5
Key materialsTimber, tile, paper
Best forA delicate garden pavilion

A raised timber pavilion with paper screens and a sweeping tiled roof on a stone base. For quiet tea.

Bandstand

CosmeticOrnate
Footprint6×6 round
Key materialsIron, timber
Best forAn ornate raised music stand

An octagonal raised platform on ornate iron columns under a peaked roof with a finial. For the band.

Sunken garden

CosmeticPool
Footprint10×9
Key materialsStone, water, flowers
Best forA walled garden below grade

A rectangular garden dropped below the lawn, with a long lily pool, beds and a stair down. Sheltered.

Rose arbour walk

CosmeticArches
Footprint11×4 walk
Key materialsIron, roses
Best forAn arched walk smothered in roses

A run of arched iron hoops over a path, draped in climbing roses. Walk through a tunnel of bloom.

Topiary menagerie

CosmeticAnimals
Footprint9×5
Key materialsClipped shrubs
Best forHedges clipped into beasts

Shrubs trained into a bird, a horse and a curl, in matching planters. A whimsical formal touch.

16 — Going up · 23 builds

Towers & verticals

Tall things for sight, sound and show: watchtowers, clock and bell towers, observatories and spires.

Tall stone watchtower

LookoutLandmark
Footprint4×4 × very tall
Key materialsStone, timber
Best forA high stone lookout with a covered top

A tapered stone shaft under a roofed watch room with overhang. Height buys sightlines; the roof keeps the watch dry.

Clock tower

CivicClock face
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsStone, timber
Best forA town clock visible across the square

A square stone tower with a clock face decal near the top under a steep cap. Site it where the whole town sees it.

Bell tower

BelfryOpen top
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsStone, timber
Best forAn open belfry hung with a bell

A stone shaft topped by an open arched belfry housing a bell. Open sides let the sound carry across the valley.

Observatory

DomeAstronomy
Footprint5×5 round + dome
Key materialsStone, copper
Best forA round tower under a rotating dome

A round stone drum capped by a copper dome with a viewing slot. Build it dark and high, away from torch glow.

Timber siege tower

SiegeWheels
Footprint5×5 × tall
Key materialsTimber, hides
Best forA mobile assault tower on wheels

A wheeled timber tower clad in hides with a drop ramp at the top. Roll it to a wall and storm across the bridge.

Slender spire

SteepleLandmark
Footprint3×3 × very tall
Key materialsStone, slate
Best forA tall thin steeple as a landmark

A narrow stone base bursting into a tall slate spire. Pure vertical drama; tops it with a finial.

Semaphore tower

SignalRelay
Footprint3×3 × tall
Key materialsTimber, planks
Best forA signal relay with movable arms

A timber tower carrying a mast with pivoting signal arms. Chain several on hilltops to pass word fast.

Round beacon tower

BeaconBrazier
Footprint4×4 round × tall
Key materialsStone, iron
Best forA round tower carrying a fire brazier

A round stone tower topped by a railed iron brazier. Keep it stocked with fuel to fire at the first alarm.

Pigeon-post tower

MessengerDovecote
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsPlaster, timber
Best forA messenger-pigeon loft for fast word

A tall plastered loft full of nest holes with a landing shelf. Carrier birds fly word home faster than any rider.

Gate-flanking turret

TurretPepperpot
Footprint3×3 round × tall
Key materialsStone, slate
Best forA slim round turret beside a gate

A pepperpot turret with a conical cap, paired beside a gate to cover the approach. Slim, tall and watchful.

Standard tower

BannerLookout
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsStone, cloth
Best forA battlemented tower flying a banner

A square battlemented tower with a tall mast and banner. Flies your colours over the keep for all to see.

Stair tower

AccessExternal stair
Footprint4×4 + stair
Key materialsStone, timber
Best forA tower with an external climbing stair

A round stair wraps a square tower to reach the top without eating interior space. Cap it with a small watch room.

Tall donjon tower

Keep towerTurreted
Footprint5×5 × very tall
Key materialsStone, slate
Best forA soaring keep-tower with a turreted crown

A slim battered keep-tower rising to a corbelled, turreted crown. All the height of a donjon on a tiny footprint.

Astronomical observatory

Rotating domeTelescope
Footprint6×6 round + dome
Key materialsStone, copper, brass
Best forA drum tower under a rotating slotted dome

A round drum carries a copper dome on a runner ring with an open slot and a brass tube poking out. Turn it to any star.

Grand clock tower

CivicTwin clocks
Footprint5×5 × very tall
Key materialsStone, copper, gold
Best forA grand civic clock with a lantern crown

A tall stone tower with clock faces on two sides under a colonnaded lantern and copper cupola. The city's timepiece.

Campanile

BelfryFree-standing
Footprint4×4 × very tall
Key materialsBrick, stone
Best forA free-standing Italian bell tower

A tall brick campanile with stacked arched openings rising to an open belfry and a pyramid cap. Stands apart from the church.

Lighthouse-tower hybrid

BeaconWatch room
Footprint5×5 round × very tall
Key materialsStone, glass
Best forA defended watch tower doubling as a sea light

A fortified round tower with a battlemented watch gallery below a glazed lantern. Guards the coast and lights the channel.

Watch spire

LookoutOpen gallery
Footprint4×4 × very tall
Key materialsStone, timber, slate
Best forA lean lookout spire with a railed gallery

A slim shaft topped by an open railed gallery under a tall slate spire. Climb it for the longest sightline around.

Signal mast tower

FlagsYardarms
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsTimber, stone
Best forA naval signal station hoisting flag hoists

A stone base under a tall mast rigged with yardarms and flag hoists. Run signal flags up the halyards to pass orders.

Minaret

BalconyCrescent
Footprint4×4 round × very tall
Key materialsWhitestone, copper
Best forA slender minaret with a muezzin's balcony

A very slender round shaft with a corbelled balcony under a tall conical cap and crescent finial. Pure vertical grace.

Grand pigeon tower

DovecoteTiered
Footprint6×6 round × tall
Key materialsPlaster, clay
Best forA round Persian-style dovecote tower

A fat round plaster tower banded with hundreds of nest holes and a ring of landing ledges. Birds carry word and dung enriches the fields.

Siege belfry

SiegeAssault ramp
Footprint5×6 × very tall
Key materialsTimber, hides, iron
Best forA towering wheeled assault belfry with archer decks

A tall many-storey siege belfry on iron-shod rollers, hide-clad against fire, with archer ports and a top drop-ramp.

Belfry gatehouse

BellGate arch
Footprint5×4 × tall
Key materialsStone, copper
Best forA bell-topped tower spanning a road arch

A tall stone tower pierced by a road arch at its foot and crowned with an open belfry. Rings the hours over the gate.

17 — By the sea · 23 builds

Coastal & naval

Where land meets water: lighthouses, piers, boathouses, shipyards, fish racks and harbour cranes.

Tall lighthouse

BeaconStriped
Footprint5×5 round × very tall
Key materialsWhitewashed stone, glass
Best forA towering striped sea light

A very tall banded round tower under a glazed lantern. Build it on the outermost point you can defend.

Stone harbour pier

QuayBollards
Footprint10×4 + water
Key materialsStone, iron
Best forA solid stone quay with mooring bollards

A heavy stone pier walking out into deep water, lined with iron bollards. Tie large vessels alongside to load.

Long timber jetty

PilingsWalkway
Footprint12 reach
Key materialsPosts, planks
Best forA long piled walk out to deep mooring

Plank decking on tall pilings reaching far over shallows to deep water. Moor a boat or fish off the end.

Boathouse over water

BerthRoofed
Footprint6×5 over water
Key materialsPosts, timber, shingles
Best forA roofed berth open to the sea

A gabled shed standing on pilings with an open water bay for a boat. Back wall on the bank, front on posts.

Ship slipway

ShipyardHull
Footprint10×6 ramp
Key materialsTimber, ribs
Best forA sloped slip with a hull under construction

A timber ramp running into the water bearing a half-planked hull on its keel blocks. Launch by sliding it down.

Fish-drying racks

FishCuring
Footprint8×5
Key materialsPoles, line
Best forOpen rows of racks for drying the catch

Rows of pole frames hung with split fish to wind-dry. Site them in the breeze above the high-tide line.

Dockside crane

CargoTreadwheel
Footprint5×5 + jib
Key materialsTimber, rope
Best forA treadwheel crane lifting cargo onto the quay

A timber treadwheel crane with a swinging jib over the quay edge. Walk the wheel to hoist barrels from a hold.

Breakwater mole

StoneHarbour
Footprint12 length
Key materialsStone, rubble
Best forA rubble mole sheltering the anchorage

A long sloped stone mole takes the swell so the water behind stays calm. Top it with a walkway and a small light.

Raised fishing platform

StiltsNet
Footprint5×5 over water
Key materialsPosts, planks
Best forA railed deck on stilts with a lift net

A railed plank deck on tall posts with a hut and a dip net on a boom. Haul the net to scoop a catch.

Harbourmaster's house

CustomsOffice
Footprint6×5 + steps
Key materialsPlaster, timber, slate
Best forA customs office overlooking the quay

A neat two-storey office on the quay edge with a lookout window over the water. Collects dues and watches arrivals.

Careening wharf

RepairHull
Footprint8×6
Key materialsStone, timber
Best forA wharf for heeling a hull over to clean it

A low stone wharf with capstans where a hull is heeled over for scraping and tarring. Work it at low water.

Shipwright's shed

ShipyardOpen shed
Footprint8×6 open
Key materialsPosts, timber, shingles
Best forAn open shed for framing boats by the water

A tall open-sided shed where a boat is framed up under cover beside the slip. Stack timber along the back wall.

Sea fort on rocks

IslandCasemates
Footprint9×8 island
Key materialsStone, granite
Best forA round gun fort planted on a sea rock

A circular granite gun-fort sits on a tidal rock ringed by surf, tiered casemate ports facing the channel.

Martello tower

RoundGun platform
Footprint6×6 round
Key materialsBrick, stone
Best forA squat round coastal tower with a top gun

A thick-walled round Martello tower with a single traversing gun on the flat roof. Squat, near-shotproof, quick to build.

Dry dock with ship

CaissonHull
Footprint12×6 basin
Key materialsStone, timber
Best forA pumped stone basin holding a ship for repair

A stepped stone graving dock, gate shut and pumped dry, with a planked hull propped on keel blocks and shores.

Careening wharf & ship

Heeled hullTackle
Footprint10×7
Key materialsTimber, stone, rope
Best forA hull hove down on its side for cleaning

A masted hull hove fully over against the wharf by masthead tackle to a capstan, its bottom bared for scraping.

Harbour chain towers

BoomTwin towers
Footprint13 span
Key materialsStone, iron chain
Best forTwo towers closing the harbour mouth with a chain

A pair of round towers on either shore with a heavy iron boom-chain slung between to bar the harbour mouth to ships.

Tidal fish weir

TrapStakes
Footprint11×8 in water
Key materialsStakes, wattle
Best forA V-shaped stake trap that nets the ebb tide

Two converging fences of stakes and wattle funnel fish into a basket trap as the tide ebbs. Empty it at low water.

Breakwater beacon

Mole headIron light
Footprint12 length
Key materialsStone, iron
Best forA mole head crowned with a skeletal iron beacon

A stone mole runs out to a round head carrying a latticed iron beacon tower with a lantern. Marks the safe channel in.

Shipwright's slipway

Launch waysBow up
Footprint11×7 ramp
Key materialsTimber, ribs
Best forA planked hull poised bow-up on launch ways

A fully framed and half-planked hull sits bow-up on greased launch ways ready to slide stern-first into the tide.

Stilt fishing village

PilingsWalkways
Footprint11×9 over water
Key materialsPosts, thatch
Best forClustered huts on stilts joined by plank walks

Three thatched huts perch on tall pilings over the shallows, linked by plank gangways with a boat moored below.

Customs quay

Bonded storeWeigh house
Footprint10×6 quay
Key materialsStone, brick, slate
Best forA bonded warehouse and weigh house on the quay

A tall bonded warehouse with a loading hatch and hoist beam stands on the quay beside a small weigh house and crane.

Fortified watergate

Sea gatePortcullis
Footprint10×8 + water
Key materialsStone, iron
Best forA castle sea gate where boats enter under a grille

A vaulted water passage between two towers admits boats from the sea behind a raisable iron grille. Traitors' gate.

18 — Keeping it · 20 builds

Storage & logistics

Hold the surplus: warehouses, silos, granaries, woodsheds, ice houses and barrel yards.

Timber warehouse

BulkLoading hoist
Footprint9×6 + hoist
Key materialsTimber, planks, shingles
Best forStoring sawn lumber under one roof

A big gabled store with a tall loading door and a hoist beam over it for lifting bundles up top.

Woodshed

FirewoodOpen store
Footprint6×4
Key materialsPosts, planks, shingles
Best forKeeping split logs dry and aired

An open-fronted lean-to packed with stacked split logs. Off the ground and under cover to season.

Cooperage store

BarrelsCasks
Footprint6×5
Key materialsPlaster, timber, barrels
Best forStoring and stacking finished casks

A barrel store with casks racked outside and a workshop within. Keep them shaded so staves don't shrink.

Dutch hay barn

HayOpen sides
Footprint8×6
Key materialsPosts, a high roof, hay
Best forStoring loose hay under a roof

A roof on tall posts with no walls, piled high with hay. Air moves through so the crop won't rot.

Grain silo cluster

Bulk grainCylinders
Footprint8×5
Key materialsPlaster/metal, timber
Best forStoring grain across several bins

Three round bins side by side, each domed and fed from the top. Spread the harvest to cut spoilage.

Large ice house

Cold storeBuried
Footprint7×6 mound
Key materialsStone, soil, timber
Best forHolding bulk ice through summer

A deep stone pit under an earth mound with a north-facing porch. Big enough to stock a whole estate.

Root clamp

RootsSoil mound
Footprint8×3 mound
Key materialsStraw, soil
Best forOverwintering roots outdoors

A long ridge of roots layered with straw and capped with soil. Cheap winter store; open one end at a time.

Salt store

SaltDry shed
Footprint5×4
Key materialsStone, plaster, slate
Best forKeeping salt bone dry

A small, tight stone shed with a slate roof and raised floor. Salt draws damp, so seal it well.

Open drying shed

DryingSlatted
Footprint7×5
Key materialsPosts, slats, shingles
Best forAir-drying herbs, fish or tobacco

A roof over slatted racks with open sides. Wind passes through and dries whatever hangs from the rails.

Stone cold-cellar

Cool storeEntrance
Footprint5×5 entrance
Key materialsStone, timber doors
Best forA walk-in cellar dug into stone

A stone-arched porch set into an earth bank, with a heavy door over the steps. The mass keeps it steady cold.

Tithe barn

GrainHuge gable
Footprint11×6
Key materialsStone, oak, thatch
Best forStoring the parish grain tithe

An immense aisled barn with great cart-doors and a steep thatch. Buttressed stone to bear the harvest.

Stilted storehouse

GranaryRaised
Footprint6×5 raised
Key materialsPosts, planks, shingles
Best forKeeping grain off damp and vermin

A timber store lifted on staddle posts with rat-guards. The gap below airs the floor and stops the vermin.

Coal staithes

CoalRaised bins
Footprint9×5 + chute
Key materialsTimber, coal, a chute
Best forLoading coal down a chute to carts

Raised timber bins fed from above, with a chute that drops coal straight into waiting carts or boats.

Warehouse row

GoodsTerrace
Footprint10×5
Key materialsBrick, timber, slate
Best forA terrace of bonded warehouses

A run of tall brick warehouses sharing party walls, each with its own loading door and hoist. Wharf-side bulk.

Cooper's barrel yard

CasksStacked
Footprint8×6
Key materialsPosts, staves, casks
Best forSeasoning and stacking finished casks

An open yard of casks stacked in pyramids under a lean-to, with stave bundles drying along the rail.

Ice harvest house

IcePond
Footprint8×7 by pond
Key materialsTimber, soil, a pond
Best forCutting and storing pond ice

A turf-banked ice store beside the cutting pond, with a ramp to slide blocks straight off the water.

Salt store house

SaltBulk dry
Footprint7×5
Key materialsStone, slate, raised floor
Best forBulk-storing salt bone dry

A long stone store on a raised floor with few openings and a tight slate roof. Salt sulks in any damp.

Drying loft

DryingLouvres
Footprint6×5 + loft
Key materialsPlaster, timber, louvres
Best forAir-drying upstairs through louvres

A two-storey store whose louvred loft lets a through-draught dry the crop while the ground floor stays dry.

Hop kiln store

HopsPressing
Footprint7×5
Key materialsPlaster, timber, pockets
Best forPressing dried hops into pockets

A storey for pressing dried hops into tall sacking pockets, ranked ready for the brewer's cart.

Root-cellar bank

RootsBanked
Footprint7×6 bank
Key materialsStone, soil, timber
Best forA cellar dug into a turf bank

A stone-faced doorway set into a long turf bank with a vented ridge. The earth holds it cool and frost-free.

19 — Master works · 22 builds

Advanced contraptions

Big, complex, mechanical: powered mill complexes, ore washeries, crane gantries, clockworks and gear halls.

Windmill mill complex

WindComplex
Footprint10×7 + tower
Key materialsStone tower, a gear hall
Best forA full milling operation off one big mill

A tower mill with an attached gear hall and grain store. The cap drives a shaft down into the work hall.

Sawmill complex

WaterLumber
Footprint11×7 + wheel
Key materialsTimber hall, a waterwheel
Best forBulk plank cutting off a waterwheel

An overshot wheel drives a frame saw in the long hall, with a log deck and plank stacks alongside.

Ore washery

WaterSorting
Footprint9×6 over water
Key materialsTimber launders, sluices
Best forWashing crushed ore over flowing water

Crushed ore is washed down stepped sluices over a pond; the heavy metal drops out and waste runs off.

Treadwheel crane

LiftingManual
Footprint6×5 × tall
Key materialsTimber, a great wheel
Best forHoisting heavy stone on a build site

A walker inside the great wheel reels in the rope to lift loads. The classic cathedral-site crane.

Gantry crane

LiftingFrame
Footprint8×5 frame
Key materialsHeavy timber, a trolley
Best forMoving loads along a fixed beam

A trolley and hoist ride a beam between two tall legs. Spans a yard so loads move the length of the gantry.

Drawbridge

GateCounterweight
Footprint8×6 + moat
Key materialsStone gatehouse, chains
Best forA lifting span across a moat

Chains over the gatehouse raise the timber deck against a counterweight. Drop it to cross, raise it to seal.

Clockwork tower

GearsTall
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsStone, a gear face, a bell
Best forA driven clock and bell over a square

A weight-driven gear train turns the face and strikes the bell on the hour. A landmark and a town timekeeper.

Multi-forge bellows

ForgePowered
Footprint9×6
Key materialsStone hearths, a cam shaft
Best forSeveral forges fed by one drive shaft

A row of forges share a powered shaft that works each bellows in turn. A small smithing production line.

Blast-furnace complex

IronHoist
Footprint8×7 + hoist
Key materialsStone stack, a charging hoist
Best forContinuous iron smelting at scale

A tall stone stack charged from a hoist platform, with bellows and a tap house at the base.

Foundry hall

CastingChimneys
Footprint10×7
Key materialsBrick, stone, tall flues
Best forMelting and casting metal in volume

A long brick hall with crucible furnaces and twin chimneys. Cast molds line the floor under the smoke hoods.

Trip-hammer battery

ForgingPowered
Footprint9×5 + wheel
Key materialsTimber, a cam shaft, anvils
Best forA row of hammers off one waterwheel

One drive shaft works several trip hammers in sequence over their anvils. Heavy, fast bulk forging.

Grain-mill complex

WaterMilling
Footprint11×7 + wheel
Key materialsStone mill, a granary, a wheel
Best forMilling and storing grain at scale

A waterwheel mill joined to a tall granary, with sack hoists in the loft. The full grain-to-flour works.

Grand mill complex

WaterTowers
Footprint13×8 + tower
Key materialsStone halls, a tower, a wheel
Best forA walled multi-hall milling estate

A tall watch tower, a long mill hall and a gabled granary share one race and wheel. The estate's whole milling works.

Ore stamp battery

CrushingPowered
Footprint9×6 + wheel
Key materialsTimber frame, a stamp row, a wheel
Best forA wall of stamps pulverising ore

A cammed shaft lifts a long row of heavy iron-shod stamps that drop into ore boxes. The wheel hammers them in turn.

Foundry & hoist

CastingCrane
Footprint10×8 + crane
Key materialsBrick hall, a jib crane, a flue
Best forCasting heavy work served by a crane

A brick casting hall with a slewing jib crane in the yard to swing crucibles and heavy moulds over the floor.

Crane gantry yard

LiftingYard
Footprint12×8 yard
Key materialsTwin timber gantries, a trolley
Best forA timber yard spanned by travelling cranes

Two parallel timber gantries carry a cross-trolley over a stacked yard, so loads travel anywhere in the bay.

Automaton hall

ClockworkDisplay
Footprint8×6 + tower
Key materialsStone hall, a gear face, figures
Best forA clockwork jack show on the hour

A weight train drives a gear face and a parade of striking figures across the gallery. A marvel over the market.

Chute works

WaterConveyor
Footprint11×7 over water
Key materialsTrestles, a bucket chain, chutes
Best forA bucket conveyor lifting over a pond

An endless bucket chain climbs a trestle to a head house, then chutes the load down across the water. Wheel-driven lift.

Forge bellows hall

ForgePowered
Footprint11×7 + wheel
Key materialsStone hall, twin bellows, a wheel
Best forA big smithy on a wheel-blown blast

Two great water-blown bellows feed a hall of hearths through one shaft. Twin flues carry the smoke; the wheel drives all.

Mine drainage works

PumpingWater
Footprint10×7 + wheel
Key materialsHeadframe, a wheel, pump rods
Best forA waterwheel pumping a mine dry

A big waterwheel works a rod-train across the yard to a timber headframe, lifting water up the shaft in stages.

Cascade watermill

WaterStepped
Footprint12×8 stepped
Key materialsStone mills, three wheels, a leat
Best forThree mills stepping down one falling leat

One leat drops through three small mills in series, each wheel taking its turn before the water falls to the next.

Twin treadwheel crane

LiftingManual
Footprint8×6 × tall
Key materialsTimber tower, twin tread wheels, a jib
Best forA heavy site crane worked by two wheels

Two great wheels on one axle double the haul on a tall slewing jib. The big version of the cathedral-site crane.

20 — Out in the wild · 18 builds

Wilderness & utility

Out-camps and odd jobs: hunting blinds, fishing huts, trapper cabins, lookouts, signposts and cairns.

Raised hunting blind

ElevatedLadder
Footprint3×3 raised
Key materialsPosts, planks, a ladder
Best forWatching a game trail from height

A small enclosed box on tall posts with a ladder up. Slit windows watch the trail; you stay hidden.

Tree stand

Around a trunkPlatform
Footprint5×5
Key materialsA tree, planks, rails
Best forA perch built around a living tree

A railed plank platform wrapped around a trunk, reached by pegs. Cheap height with natural cover.

Fishing hut on a bank

WatersideJetty
Footprint4×4 + jetty
Key materialsPlanks, posts, thatch
Best forA tiny base by good water

A one-room hut at the water's edge with a short jetty out over it. Dry your nets on the rail.

Trapper's cabin

TinyPelt rack
Footprint4×4 + rack
Key materialsLogs, stone, hides
Best forA remote one-man out-camp

A tiny log cabin with a stone chimney and a rack of stretched pelts drying outside the door.

Lookout platform

Tall postsRailed deck
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsPosts, planks, rails
Best forA high open watch over the land

Four tall braced posts carry a railed open deck, reached by a long ladder. Sightlines, not shelter.

Signpost

WayfindingArms
Footprint2×2
Key materialsA post, plank arms
Best forMarking a junction or route home

A stout post with pointing plank arms at different heights. Carve distances and aim each arm true.

Trail cairn

Stacked stonesMarker
Footprint2×2
Key materialsStacked stones
Best forMarking a path across bare ground

A pile of stones tapering to a point, big enough to spot from afar. Stack a chain to mark a route.

Forager's drying rack

PreserveOpen frame
Footprint5×3
Key materialsPoles, cord, a tarp
Best forDrying herbs, meat and fish in camp

A pole frame hung with strips and bundles under a small rain cover. Air-dries the day's foraging.

Bird hide

Low screenConcealed
Footprint5×3
Key materialsPlanks, brush, a slit
Best forWatching wildlife unseen at ground level

A low screen wall with a viewing slit and a brush-topped roof. Sit behind it and stay out of sight.

Wilderness shrine

RoadsideSmall
Footprint3×3
Key materialsStone, timber, an offering
Best forA quiet marker at a special spot

A little stone plinth with a gabled timber canopy over a niche. A landmark with a touch of reverence.

Raised hunting tower

Tall postsEnclosed cabin
Footprint4×4 × tall
Key materialsPosts, planks, shingles
Best forWatching far over open ground

A small enclosed cabin on tall braced posts with a ladder up. Slit windows on every side to watch.

Observation deck on poles

Open platformRails
Footprint5×5 × tall
Key materialsPoles, planks, rails
Best forAn open high lookout over a marsh

An open railed deck on tall poles with cross-bracing, reached by a long ladder. Sightlines, no walls.

Wild apiary

BeehivesForage
Footprint5×4
Key materialsSkeps, a bench, a fence
Best forKeeping bees at a forest edge

A row of woven straw skeps on a low bench under a small rain roof. A light fence keeps wildlife out.

Charcoal burner's camp

Earth kilnSmoke
Footprint6×6
Key materialsStacked wood, earth, a hut
Best forBurning wood down to charcoal

A turf-covered earth mound smouldering wood into charcoal, with a small turf hut to watch it overnight.

Fishing weir hut

In the streamTrap
Footprint6×6 + weir
Key materialsStakes, hurdles, a hut
Best forTrapping fish in a running stream

A stake-and-hurdle weir funnels fish into a trap by a tiny stilt hut over the water. Check it at dawn.

Ranger cabin

Patrol basePorch
Footprint6×5 + porch
Key materialsLogs, stone, metal roof
Best forA warden's base in the backcountry

A neat log cabin with a covered porch, stone chimney and a metal roof. A small woodshed leans on the end.

Message cairn & signpost

WayfindingBox
Footprint3×3
Key materialsStacked stones, a post
Best forA drop-box and marker on a route

A tall stone cairn beside a signpost with a small weatherproof message box. Leave word, take a bearing.

Forest shrine

Torii gateQuiet
Footprint5×4
Key materialsTimber, stone, a lantern
Best forA reverent marker on a forest path

A red torii gate before a small raised shrine with a steep roof and a stone lantern. A quiet waypoint.

The Pattern Book
Part of The Drifter's Almanac, an unofficial fan-made guide to Vintage Story (v1.22.3).
Vintage Story is by Anego Studios. This guide is not affiliated with them.
Companion pages:
· The Architect (decorative technique)
· The Homestead (course-by-course plans)

The illustrations are stylised isometric massing studies meant to show shape and proportion, not exact block counts or game textures. Sizes, materials and recipes are config-/version-dependent. Your in-game Handbook and creative palette are the final word.
